_comment: Term -> short definition; condensed for quick reference. Full prose lives on basic-terms.html.

terms.def: Anything that holds economic value and can be owned to produce a benefit — cash, stocks, bonds, mutual funds, real estate.
Ownership in a company. Buying a stock means owning a small part of the company's assets and earnings.
A system for the organized buying and selling of stocks on stock exchanges.
The profit from selling an asset for more than its purchase price.
Selling an asset for less than you paid. Losses can offset gains, reducing your tax burden.
The net combination of all capital losses and gains.
The collection of investments held by a person or institution; diversified portfolios mix asset classes to manage risk.
Spreading investments across different assets and sectors so no single risk can sink you.
Earning returns on both your original investment and the returns it already produced. Growth accelerates over time.
An investment gaining value between when you buy it and when you sell it.
The account that holds your shares electronically in India. Minors can have one operated by a guardian.
Systematic Investment Plan — investing a fixed amount every month instead of one lump sum.
Compound Annual Growth Rate — your total return converted into a per-year rate so different investments can be compared.
Short Term Capital Gains — profit on assets held a short time; taxed at 20% (Budget 2024).
Long Term Capital Gains — profit on assets held longer; taxed at 12.5% above ₹1.25 lakh (Budget 2024).
Your asset went up but you haven't sold — no tax is due until you sell.
Practising investing with fake money on a simulator before risking real money.
An investment account for a minor, managed by a parent/guardian; the minor owns the investments.
Permanent Account Number — the tax ID needed (via your guardian, if you're a minor) to invest in India.
Rising cost of living. If returns are 5% and inflation is 3%, your real return is only 2%.
